name: Ma-po's Bean Curd
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the tofu into 3/4 inch cubes.
2. Cook the tofu in boiling water for a while.
3. Heat oil in a wok. Quickly stir-fry the ground pork with Chili Nam Yuey and ginger until the pork is well cooked.
4. Add the soup stock, tofu, soy sauce, and wine to the wok. Simmer over low heat until there is almost no liquid left.
5. Stir in the cornstarch paste and remove from heat.
6. Sprinkle with sesame oil, ground pepper, and minced spring onion. Serve.
Note:
This dish is considered authentic only if it tastes hot, spicy, and tender all at the same time. The original recipe called for ground beef; most places now use pork. Mrs. Chen Ma-Po, a widow known as Pockmarked Chen, gained fame and recognition when she created this dish in her tofu stall about 100 years ago. The legend lives on forever!
